expectations.text: has_all_sections: Spec has ALL required section headings including ## Acceptance Criteria
expectations.evidence: Missing ## Acceptance Criteria section entirely. All other required sections present (Problem, Solution, Scope, Design Decisions, Failure Modes, Risks & Open Questions, Alternatives Considered, Verification).

expectations.text: no_code_fences: No triple-backtick code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: No triple-backtick code fences appear anywhere in the spec. No Implementation Notes section exists either.

expectations.text: no_ts_interfaces: Design Decisions must not contain 'interface ', type annotations, or TS syntax
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions contains 'The options interface accepts the dashboard object' — the literal string 'interface ' is present. Also references 'ChatTool array' and 'ToolResult' which are TypeScript type names.

expectations.text: no_json_shapes: Design Decisions must not contain JSON-like shapes or data structure definitions
expectations.evidence: No JSON-like shapes, curly braces defining structures, or data structure definitions found. Panel metadata is described in prose (ID, title, type, query metric names) without JSON formatting.

expectations.text: no_exact_file_paths_in_scope: In Scope must not contain file paths or file extensions
expectations.evidence: In Scope section contains only behavioral descriptions. No 'src/' paths, no '.ts'/'.tsx' extensions. File paths only appear in AGENTS.md Updates section at the bottom.

expectations.text: spec_under_120_lines: Total lines excluding Implementation Notes must be ≤120
expectations.evidence: Spec is approximately 88 lines total. No Implementation Notes section exists, so all lines counted. Well under 120 threshold.

expectations.text: bold_topic_sentences: >80% of Design Decisions paragraphs open with bold sentence stating decision as fact
expectations.evidence: All 8 Design Decisions paragraphs open with **bold** declarative sentences: context hook serialization, tools hook pattern, tab navigation, time range, edit mode/save, template variables, fullscreen toggling, export. 8/8 = 100%.

expectations.text: implementer_can_start: Spec provides enough detail for a developer to begin implementing
expectations.evidence: Names established patterns (three-hook pattern, useAlertsTools pattern, useRegisterChatTools), state management (Zustand view store, react-query), each tool's behavior in detail, confirmation gating policy, error handling strategy, and existing mechanisms to follow.

expectations.text: no_implementation_leaks: Design Decisions must not prescribe HOW at the React hook level
expectations.evidence: Prescribes specific React hooks: 'a useMemo returning a stable ChatTool array', 'a useLatestRef wrapping the options object', 'useCallback with all relevant dependencies'. These are hook-level implementation prescriptions, not module/pattern references.

expectations.text: failure_modes_are_policy: >50% of failure modes are genuine policy decisions the team could disagree on
expectations.evidence: All 3 failure modes are policy decisions: (1) reject invalid variable values vs silently accept — policy, (2) return failure when dashboard not loaded vs queue tools — policy, (3) fail on ambiguous title match vs pick first — policy. 3/3 = 100%.

expectations.text: scope_is_behavioral: >80% of In Scope items are behavioral capabilities, not file/component names
expectations.evidence: All 10 In Scope items describe user-facing capabilities or behavioral features (context providing state, tools for navigation/time range/fullscreen/edit/save/variables/export, suggested prompts, panel metric summaries). 10/10 = 100%.

expectations.text: has_acceptance_criteria: ## Acceptance Criteria exists with 4-8 behavioral checkbox items
expectations.evidence: No ## Acceptance Criteria section exists. The spec has ## Verification with Automated and Manual subsections, but these are verification steps, not acceptance criteria as a distinct section.

expectations.text: verification_agent_actionable: Must have Agent-Verifiable subsection with action→outcome checks
expectations.evidence: Verification has ### Automated (build/lint/test) and ### Manual subsections only. No ### Agent-Verifiable subsection. All non-build behavioral checks are under Manual with no agent-verifiable counterpart.

expectations.text: has_appetite: Header must contain **Appetite:** field with real time-budget value
expectations.evidence: Header contains Date, Status, Amendments, Superseded-by, and Ticket fields. No **Appetite:** field present anywhere in the header.

expectations.text: has_boundary_tiers: Design Decisions must contain at least 2 boundary-tier statements (Always/Ask First/Never or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Contains boundary-tier equivalents: 'Edit mode and save are confirmation-gated tools' (Ask First tier), 'that tool does not require confirmation' for exit edit (Always-allow tier), 'This tool is read-only and does not require confirmation' for export (Always-allow tier), 'Tool handlers never throw' (Never tier). At least 3 boundary statements present.
